response:
To determine how many bottles Brittany needs for a leap year, we first calculate the total number of washes she performs. Since a leap year has 366 days and she washes her hair every other day, she will wash her hair 183 times. Multiplying 183 washes by the 1/4 ounce of shampoo used per wash results in a total consumption of 45.75 ounces. Given that each bottle contains 14 ounces, dividing the total amount needed by the bottle size shows she requires approximately 3.27 bottles, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to ensure she has enough for the entire year at [store_name].

After purchasing the necessary bottles, Brittany should plan to keep the extra product in a safe place like her [bathroom_cabinet] for future use. Since she will use 45.75 ounces out of the 56 ounces provided by four bottles, she will have 10.25 ounces remaining after the leap year concludes. This surplus ensures she is prepared for the start of the next calendar year without needing an immediate trip to the store. By managing her inventory this way, she can effectively track her usage and avoid running out of shampoo unexpectedly.
